source.title: The organization of recent and remote memories

confidence_rationale: Canonical review establishing that consolidation is a time-extended, substrate-redistributive process in which initially hippocampus-dependent memories become progressively cortex-dependent. This supports the MECH-261 architectural commitment that different operating modes engage different substrates (hippocampal during acquisition/retrieval, cortical during consolidated use). Confidence moderated because the review frames consolidation in terms of recent-vs-remote anatomy rather than the discrete categorical operating-mode variable MECH-261 posits; the biology is more continuous than the mode framing.
failure_signatures: Systems consolidation is a continuous, graded process (hippocampal-cortical interplay changes progressively over days-to-months), not a discrete mode flip -- a MECH-261 implementation that uses a strictly categorical operating_mode variable will miss the graded hippocampal-cortical handoff documented here.

The review emphasises that consolidation occurs largely offline (sleep-dependent), while MECH-261 must also cover waking reactivation and online propagation -- over-reliance on this paper biases the design toward offline-only consolidation.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Memory traces initially depend on the medial temporal lobe / hippocampus and become progressively reorganised into neocortical networks over a time course of days to months, with sleep-related hippocampal-cortical dialogue driving this reorganisation.
mapping.ree_translation: The substrate that stores a viability-map trace shifts over repeated consolidation opportunities: initial acquisition writes are hippocampal (MECH-092 waking SWR, ARC-038 map substrate), later durable storage is cortical (ARC-039 grid/cortical loop). MECH-261's claim that gates differ by operating mode (external_task vs internal_replay vs offline_consolidation) is necessary if the write substrate itself differs by mode -- otherwise the mode variable has no downstream consequence to gate.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Frankland and Bontempi describe the anatomical redistribution of memory traces but do not posit a discrete operating-mode variable that gates which substrate is eligible to write. The REE translation reads categorical mode gating into a continuous biological process. The bridge is that if hippocampal-cortical dialogue can proceed in offline consolidation but is suppressed during external-task commitment (as downstream literature suggests), then a mode variable is a reasonable abstraction of the biology -- but this paper alone does not license the categorical form.
mapping.source_context: Narrative review of rodent and human lesion, imaging, and electrophysiology studies spanning the 1990s-early 2000s. Hippocampal-cortical consolidation framework (standard consolidation theory and multiple trace theory both discussed).

confidence_components.notes: High source quality (Nature Reviews, one of the foundational systems-consolidation reviews, ~5000 citations). Mapping fidelity moderated because the paper does not use a mode-variable framing; it describes anatomical redistribution. Transfer risk is the risk that treating graded hippocampal-cortical handoff as a discrete mode gate oversimplifies the biology.
